etimesExplained: How these 5 animals sense danger before humansAnimals often sense danger long before humans, a survival instinct honed by superior senses. Dogs detect high-frequency sounds and vibrations, while elephants feel ground tremors. Birds react to air pressure changes, snakes sense vibrations, and cats possess acute hearing. These creatures' heightened awareness offers early warnings, a stark contrast to human reliance on visible cues.
